channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Pokemon TCG Pocket Mega Shine Booster Pack Is All About Shiny Pokemon
item.content:encoded:
The Pokemon Company and DeNA have announced a new Pokemon TCG Pocket Themed Booster Pack expansion, and it's all about shiny Pokemon, as the name suggests.

Mega Shine is a Themed Booster Pack rather than a full-blooded expansion, but you can still expect lots of new cards to collect, and naturally, they'll all come with sparkling new art for you to enjoy as well. It's launching on March 26th.

The debut trailer for Mega Shine shows off a number of the new cards you can expect, including new ex cards for Mega Gengar and its shiny variant, as well as a gorgeous new Mega Charizard ex card that has some frankly breathtaking artwork to go with it.

It's not just all about the Megas, though; several normal shiny variants are also shown during the trailer, including for the likes of Pineco, Dragonair, and Zorua, along with a full art card for Empoleon.

In short, it looks like there will be lots of cards for you to collect in this pack, so whether you're a shiny aficionado or just someone who likes to look at pretty Pokemon pictures (no judgement), you'll probably find something to love here.

Pokemon TCG Pocket looks to be going from strength to strength, too; back in October, The Pokemon Company revealed that the game had passed 150 million downloads, and it's almost certainly racked up even more since then as well.

You can check out the debut trailer for Pokemon TCG Pocket's Mega Shine Themed Booster Pack, which shows off some of the glorious artwork you'll be able to admire when the set launches, right here.

Pokemon TCG Pocket's Mega Shine booster expansion launches on March 26th. The game is available to play for free right now on Android and iOS. Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Pokemon TCG Pocket.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/pokemon-tcg-pocket-mega-shine-booster-pack
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vampire Crawlers Release Date Brings Deckbuilding Goodness Next Month
item.content:encoded:
Solo dev Luca Galante, better known as the indie studio Poncle, has announced the release date for "turboturn" roguelike deckbuilder Vampire Crawlers, and it's coming very soon indeed.

As revealed in a typically tongue-in-cheek trailer, Vampire Crawlers will be arriving across PC and consoles on April 21st, and it'll be playable on Game Pass on day one as well (assuming you're subscribed to the right tier, of course).

Vampire Crawlers is also due to arrive on mobile devices at some point in the future, but Galante says this release will happen "at a later date" and promises to keep players posted on what's going on with the mobile versions of the game.

If you're not familiar with Vampire Crawlers, it's a spinoff of the hugely popular bullet heaven game Vampire Survivors. Our own Don was impressed by the demo, including it in his top Steam Next Fest demos for this past February (and it's still available now if you want to try it!).

Poncle describes the game as a "casual, turn-based deckbuilder with roguelite elements" in which your goal is to "deal world-ending combos and blitz through infested dungeons". Arcade Paradise and Vostok Inc. developer Nosebleed Interactive is helping out with this one as well.

Even if you're not a fan of the usually methodical pace of roguelite deckbuilders, Poncle says you can either choose to "take your time and be tactical" or to throw caution to the wind and "play turns as fast as you humanly can".

You can check out the new release date trailer for Vampire Crawlers below. It's well worth a watch and should give you at least a chuckle or two.

Vampire Crawlers comes to PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch on April 21st. Versions for Android and iOS are also in the works. Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Poncle.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/vampire-crawlers-release-date
